title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SWEET HOME BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 799 Long Street Sweet Home, OR 97386 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm (541) 967-3888 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 967-3888 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to register FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Linn County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located near the intersection of Highway 20 and Highway 228 in Sweet Home past the East Linn Museum and across from the Sweet Home Inn. Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Nutrition Education * Families with Infants/Toddlers Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs * At Risk Families Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SWEET HOME
